capabilities: Ensure schema-based transformations are tailored to specific tool requirements, enhancing compatibility and performance.

procedure.action: Specify schema requirements by analyzing tool documentation and extracting input specifications by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Invoke JSON Schema to model input transformations, ensuring compliance with tool-specific constraints by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Invoke transformation logic using a library like 'ajv' to validate inputs against the defined schema following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

Test transformed inputs with a suite of unit tests covering edge cases and invalid data scenarios using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Release the transformation module in a staging environment and conduct integration tests with the target tool using blue-green deployment strategy; run smoke_test() against new instance before switching traffic; assert smoke_test_pass_rate >= 1.0 before promoting.

summary: Specialize schema-based input transformation by integrating domain-specific constraints and examples, ensuring seamless tool interfacing in software architecture.
title: Schema-Based Input Transformation for Tool Interfacing

failureConditions: Incompatible input formats lead to tool errors; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Transformation logic fails under edge cases; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Schema updates are not propagated, causing integration failures.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: Input transformations pass all unit tests with 100% coverage; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Integration tests confirm compatibility with the target tool; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Schema changes trigger automated validation workflows; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
